_posts/Math/Algebraic_Structures/ko/2024.verify_verdict:
Now I have a complete picture. Let me summarize the findings:

**Root cause of count difference**: KO uses `$$...$$` for ALL math (both inline and display), while EN uses `$...$` for inline math and `$$...$$` for display math. The validator only counted `$$...$$` blocks, giving ko=153, en=8. This is a formatting difference, not a content difference.

**Actual math content comparison** (counting all math regardless of delimiter):
- KO: 156 math expressions
- EN: 151 math expressions
- Difference: 5 extra expressions in KO

**The 5 extra expressions in KO are all due to rephrasing/sentence structure differences**:

1. **def1**: 2 extra `A` in KO — KO has Korean subscript glosses `<sub>왼쪽 $$A$$-가군</sub>` and `<sub>오른쪽 $$A$$-가군</sub>` that EN drops (English doesn't need translation glosses). SAFE-REPHRASE.

2. **def2**: 1 extra `A` in KO — KO has `<sub>유한생성 $$A$$-가군</sub>` that EN drops. SAFE-REPHRASE.

3. **prop8 proof**: 1 extra `\Hom_{\lMod{A}}(M,N)` in KO — KO sentence structure: "$$\Hom_{\lMod{A}}(M,N)$$의 임의의 두 원소..." explicitly names the set before "any two elements of". EN rephrases: "The sum $v+w\in\Hom_{\lMod{A}}(M,N)$ of any two elements...". The same mathematical objects are referenced. SAFE-REPHRASE.

4. **After prop8 proof**: 1 extra `\cdot` in KO — KO says "으로 정의된 $$\cdot$$이" (the operation $$\cdot$$ defined by...). EN says "the operation defined by... gives..." without explicitly naming `\cdot` as a standalone symbol. The operation is still defined the same way. SAFE-REPHRASE.

**No mathematical content was lost, altered, or added.** The only differences are:
- Korean translation subscripts (glosses) that don't exist in English
- Sentence structure differences in how mathematical objects are referenced
- One standalone `\cdot` reference vs. implicit reference

All differences are SAFE-REPHRASE.

VERDICT: safe
COUNTS: ko=153 en=8
FINDINGS:
- [global] SAFE-REPHRASE: KO uses $$...$$ for all math (inline+display); EN uses $...$ for inline and $$...$$ for display — the raw $$ block count difference is purely a delimiter convention change with zero semantic impact.
- [def1] SAFE-REPHRASE: 2 extra $$A$$ in KO come from Korean subscript glosses <sub>왼쪽 $$A$$-가군</sub> and <sub>오른쪽 $$A$$-가군</sub> that EN correctly drops (no English gloss needed).
- [def2] SAFE-REPHRASE: 1 extra $$A$$ in KO is inside the Korean subscript gloss <sub>유한생성 $$A$$-가군</sub> that EN omits.
- [prop8 proof] SAFE-REPHRASE: 1 extra $$\Hom_{\lMod{A}}(M,N)$$ in KO because Korean syntax explicitly names the set before "의 임의의 두 원소"; EN rephrases as "The sum ... of any two elements" without the standalone set reference.
- [post-prop8] SAFE-REPHRASE: 1 extra $$\cdot$$ in KO because Korean text says "으로 정의된 $$\cdot$$이" explicitly naming the operation; EN says "the operation defined by" without standalone symbol reference.

VERDICT: safe

FINDINGS:
- [def1] SAFE-REPHRASE: Korean genitive '$$x$$의 … $$U$$' reordered to English '…of $$x$$ … $$U$$ …', splitting one math-adjacent block into two inline positions; no symbols lost.
- [def2/ex3] SAFE-REPHRASE: Same grammatical reordering pattern for '$$M$$의 base를 $$\mathcal{B}$$라 했을 때' → 'letting $$\mathcal{B}$$ be a base for $$M$$' and '$$m$$-manifold $$M$$의 open subspace $$U$$' → 'open subspace $$U$$ of an $$m$$-manifold $$M$$'; block counts shift but identical symbols present.
- [ex3 proof] SAFE-REPHRASE: '$$x$$의 $$M$$에서의 열린근방 $$V$$' → 'open neighborhood $$V$$ of $$x$$ in $$M$$'; Korean postpositions cause different $$V$$/$$x$$/$$M$$ block grouping.
- [ex5 dimensions] SAFE-REPHRASE: KO bundles '$$m_1,m_2$$' in one block; EN splits to '$$m_1$$ and $$m_2$$'; same variables, same meaning.
- [ex5 basis sentence] SAFE-REPHRASE: KO '$$M_i$$의 base를 $$\mathcal{B}_i$$라 했을 때, 다음의 식 … 으로 주어지는 $$\mathcal{B}$$가' vs EN 'letting $$\mathcal{B}_i$$ be a base for $$M_i$$, the collection $$\mathcal{B}$$ given by'; sentence restructuring preserves all symbols.
- [ex5 typo fix] SAFE-REPHRASE: KO has typo '$$(x_1,x_1)\in M_1\times M_2$$'; EN corrects to '$$(x_1,x_2)\in M_1\times M_2$$'; no mathematical content lost, rather a typo is fixed.
- [half-space eq] SAFE-REPHRASE: EN adds trailing period inside display equation '$$\mathbb{H}^m=...\right\}.$$' vs KO without period; punctuation only.
- [prop6 proof typo fix] SAFE-REPHRASE: KO has malformed '$$(\pi^{-1}(U_i)_{i\in J}$$' (missing closing parenthesis); EN fixes to '$$(\pi^{-1}(U_i))_{i\in J}$$'; no content lost, typo corrected.
